mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, corporate profit margins contract due to wage suppression and reduced consumer spending, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or t-statistic for return difference is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings and depresses equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an economic expansion phase indicates strong capacity utilization and rising corporate revenues, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of accelerating INDPRO periods, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent triggers a liquidity drain from financial markets, increasing borrowing costs for corporations and forcing equity repricing downward.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive effects.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly expands corporate profit pools, creating a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105.0 during a recessionary phase signals severe output contraction that accelerates equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 105.0, or the signal fails to predict returns when CPI inflation is below 2.0 percent.
